text: The default embedding provider is local fastembed.
Nothing is ever deleted from the wiki; supersession retains every claim (keep-everything).
pc wiki doctor demotes stale guides rather than deleting them.
The capture pipeline routes each claim to exactly one topic slug.
Inject compile synthesizes dense prose with mandatory inline (file:line) citations.
Research capture writes records into the external PC memory workspace and snapshots them as immutable project-store objects.
capture_research is enabled by default.
PC_INCLUDE_TASK_RESULTS is on by default so subagent task-results are captured.
The EXTRACT granularity block is on by default in all capture arms.
Episode cards are treated as historical provenance, not current truth.
The reranking stage assigns each claim to exactly one guide or declares it NEW.
delta-EXTRACT is off by default in the live capture path.
We could add a pull-based context tool the agent queries on demand.
Maybe later we switch the cache to Redis.
We could merge stale guides instead of grouping them into topics.
We might add an uncertainty-gated injection mode.
One option is to dump everything into a single PRODUCT_MODEL.md.
We could gate inject on a reasoning model to improve correctness.
Perhaps we add a cross-agent standup board later.
We could delete agent-authored claims and keep only user claims.
Maybe we render the explicit/implicit direction tag in the briefing.
note: tag is metadata only, never rendered

text: We could enable delta-EXTRACT by default.
note: not adopted; sweet-spot keeps it off

text: One idea: a spin-3 routing pass that drops low-signal facts.
We might add a context-file dump the agent reads at session start.
